data.title: Complete Onboarding Config Parameter Coverage
data.description:
## Context
When the DSO plugin was restructured to merge /dso:project-setup into /dso:onboarding (epic 8fdd-a993), the project-setup SKILL.md was replaced with a redirect stub. The onboarding skill adopted a Socratic dialogue format but only ported a subset of the config parameter coverage. The CI autodetection wizard (task dso-bwtp, commit c09d204d) — which prompted for ci.fast_gate_job, ci.fast_fail_job, ci.test_ceil_job, ci.integration_workflow, and the merge.ci_workflow_name deprecation notice — was dropped entirely. Meanwhile, project-detect.sh already emits detection data (ci_workflow_names, db_present, version_files, stack) that onboarding Phase 1 consumes but Phase 3 doesn't fully use when generating dso-config.conf. 11 tests still point at the redirect stub and fail in CI.

## Success Criteria
1. Onboarding Phase 3 Step 2b config template includes these detection-backed keys (populated from project-detect.sh key=value output, presented to user for confirmation): ci.fast_gate_job, ci.fast_fail_job, ci.test_ceil_job, ci.integration_workflow (from ci_workflow_names), version.file_path (from version_files — single value; see SC4), stack (from detect-stack.sh output)
2. When ci_workflow_confidence=low OR ci_workflow_names contains 2+ comma-separated entries, onboarding presents a numbered list of detected workflow filenames for user to select (not free-text); when ci_workflow_confidence=high and exactly one entry, no CI question is asked
3. When existing dso-config.conf contains merge.ci_workflow_name, onboarding logs a deprecation notice and writes value to ci.workflow_name (auto-migration, no blocking prompt). When ci.workflow_name already exists, skip migration and log that merge.ci_workflow_name can be manually removed
4. When version_files contains 2+ comma-separated paths, onboarding presents a numbered selection; writes only the selected single repo-root-relative path to version.file_path
5. Key name mismatches corrected in onboarding SKILL.md AND all codebase files that read them (verified by grep returning 0 matches for old names): jira.project_key -> jira.project, design.system -> design.system_name. Also audit design.tokens_path in SKILL.md config table against validate-config.sh KNOWN_KEYS
6. These 11 test files updated to read onboarding/SKILL.md instead of project-setup/SKILL.md. The 7 Python tests pass because SC1 adds the CI keys they assert. The 4 bash tests pass with assertions matching onboarding content. CI Python Skills workflow: 0 failures

## Scenario Analysis
- Key name migration must handle existing configs (critical): detect old key names in existing dso-config.conf and actively migrate values
- CI dialogue must validate against detected filenames (high): present numbered choices from CI_WORKFLOWS, not free-text
- version_files multi-value requires disambiguation (high): surface numbered selection when multiple version files detected
- design.system_name rename requires cross-skill audit (medium): grep all read sites before renaming
- merge.ci_workflow_name both-keys-coexist (medium): auto-migration conditional on ci.workflow_name being absent
- version.file_path must be repo-root-relative (medium): resolve paths relative to repo root, not bare filenames
- Retargeted tests must include behavioral assertions (medium): at least some tests must exercise SC1-SC5 behavior, not just check file paths
- design.tokens_path not in validate-config.sh KNOWN_KEYS (medium): audit SKILL.md config table against KNOWN_KEYS

## Dependencies
None

## Approach
Extend the existing onboarding flow: Phase 1 already calls project-detect.sh — extend Phase 3 Step 2b config template to include the 7 newly consumed keys. Add CI-specific Socratic dialogue gated on ci_workflow_confidence. Fix 2 key name mismatches with grep-verified codebase audit. Retarget 11 broken tests to onboarding SKILL.md.

### Planning Intelligence Log
- Web research (Step 2.6): not triggered
- Scenario analysis (Step 2.75): triggered, then re-triggered via gate
  - Round 1: 7 scenarios surviving blue team filter (4 addressed by SC revision, 3 informational)
  - Round 2: 4 new scenarios surviving blue team filter (all incorporated)
- Practitioner-requested additional cycles: scenario analysis re-run 1 time
